content: # MISTY CORE MANDATE (Mistral Vibe -- Shared Context Hub)

## Heartbeat Protocol -- run every session, no exceptions

### Phase 1 -- Orient
1. `git pull origin master` -- get the latest state from the team.
2. **OPTIMIZED**: Run `python3 heartbeat_script.py` -- checksum cache for MISSION_CONTROL.md (Ticket #73). Only read MISSION_CONTROL.md if content changed.
3. Read `AGENTS/RULES.md` -- team rules.
4. Read `AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json` -- ALL unread messages before anything else. They may change your priorities entirely.
5. If tasks are assigned, GET `http://localhost:8090/api/collections/lessons/records?filter=status="active"` -- load team knowledge.
6. POST `http://localhost:8090/api/collections/heartbeats/records` `{"agent": "misty", "status": "working"}`

### Phase 2 -- Peer Review First
1. GET `http://localhost:8090/api/collections/tasks/records?filter=status="peer_review"`
2. For each task assigned to the fleet: post feedback comment (`type: "feedback"`) or approval (`type: "approval"`) and set status to `approved`.
3. Do NOT self-approve -- Rule #6. A different agent must approve your own work.

### Phase 3 -- Own Tasks
1. GET `http://localhost:8090/api/collections/tasks/records?filter=assigned_agent%3D%22misty%22%26%26status%3D%22todo%22` -- pick first, set `in_progress`. **IMPORTANT: Do NOT create a new task if one already exists. Only pick up existing todo tasks.**
2. Do the work.
3. POST output to `/api/collections/comments/records` `{"task_id": "...", "agent": "misty", "content": "...", "type": "output"}`
4. Set task status to `peer_review`.

### Phase 4 -- Blockers
- If blocked: POST comment `type: "question"`, mention `"@miguel"` or `"@[agent]"`.
- Set task status to `waiting_human`.

### Phase 5 -- Lessons
- If session produced reusable insight: POST `/api/collections/lessons/records` `{"title": "...", "content": "...", "category": "...", "confidence": "medium", "status": "pending_review"}`

### Phase 6 -- Sign Off
- POST heartbeat `{"agent": "misty", "status": "idle"}`
- Write session summary to `~/fleet/misty/PROGRESS.md`
- Commit and push all changes: `git add -A && git commit -m "misty: session summary" && git push`

## Team Protocols (The Shared Memory System)

1.  **Rules**: Read and follow the instructions in `AGENTS/RULES.md`.
2.  **Reporting**: Record your progress in the daily standup file located in `standups/`.
3.  **Communication**: Use clear Markdown headers and ticket IDs in your session reporting.
4.  **Action**: Commit and push your changes to ensure the next agent is up to date.

## Source of Truth

- **Context**: Refer to `AGENTS/CONTEXT/` for deep project history and architectural context.
- **Goal**: Keep the Mission Control and Standup files updated to ensure the next agent is fully up to speed.
- **Secrets Management**: NEVER look for or create `.env` files. Use `vault/agent-fetch.sh` (Unix) or `vault/agent-fetch.ps1` (Windows). See `vault/README.md` for details.

## European Model Advantage

As a Mistral model you are EU-hosted, GDPR-compliant, and open-weight. When working on customer-facing content or the `bigbearengineering.com` pitch, lean into this: customers in regulated industries (finance, health, legal) can self-host their fleet with you at the core.

path: /Users/miguelrodriguez/projects/agentic-fleet-hub/AGENTS/RULES.md
content: # Team Collaboration Rules (The Shared Memory System)

Welcome to the **Ursushoribilis Agentic Workspace**. These rules are followed by **Clau**, **Gem**, **Codi**, and **Misty**.

---

## GitHub & Commits

1.  **Branching Strategy**: Use clear, feature-based branches.
2.  **Commit Messages**: Standardize on prefixing (e.g., `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`).
3.  **Deploy Keys**: For SSH access, use the `github-clau`, `github-codi`, or `github-gem` aliases in your `~/.ssh/config`.
4.  **Action**: Every commit must be pushed immediately to ensure the next agent is up to date.
5.  **Server = repo**: If you deploy a file directly to a production server (e.g. via SSH or `scp`), you MUST commit that same file to the repo in the same session. Never leave server-only changes uncomitted. The repo is the source of truth — if it is not in git, it does not exist.

---

## Architecture & Memory

1.  **Project Root**: Every project MUST have an `ARCHITECTURE.md` file describing its core components and data flow.
2.  **README**: Every project MUST have a developer-friendly `README.md`.
3.  **Memory Updates**: If you learn something new or change an architectural pattern, update the corresponding file in `AGENTS/CONTEXT/` immediately.

---

## Kanban & Reporting

1.  **Standups**: All daily progress is reported in `standups/`.
2.  **Kanban Flow**: Ticket-based system tracked in `MISSION_CONTROL.md`.
    *   **In Progress**: Documented in the active session's standup.
    *   **Blocked**: Explicitly listed in the Blockers section of the standup.
3.  **Finalization**: A task is only "Done" when the code is pushed AND the standup is updated.
4.  **Ticket Authority**: The Ticket Status table in `MISSION_CONTROL.md` is the ONLY source of truth for open tickets. Do NOT pick up a ticket mentioned anywhere else -- examples, old standups, context docs. If it is not in that table, it does not exist for you.
5.  **Ticket Links**: Every new ticket added to the open ticket table in `MISSION_CONTROL.md` must include its GitHub issue or project URL in the notes/status columns so Flotilla can link the Kanban card back to the source item.
6.  **No Self-Approval**: An agent MUST NOT approve its own task. When you complete a task, move it to `peer_review` status and stop. A *different* agent must read the output, verify the work was actually done, and post the approval comment. If no peer is available in the same session, leave it in `peer_review` — it will be picked up at the next heartbeat. Marking your own work `approved` is a protocol violation regardless of how confident you are in the output.

---

## Secrets & Safety

1.  **Credential Protection**: Never commit secrets or API keys.
2.  **Vault Access**: Use `vault/agent-fetch.sh` (Unix) or `vault/agent-fetch.ps1` (Windows) to fetch secrets at runtime.
3.  **Communication**: Do not pass sensitive info in Markdown files.

---

## Inter-Agent Protocol (IAP)

- **Inbox**: Read `AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json` at the start of every session. Messages from teammates may change your priorities.
- **Send**: Add a message object to `AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json` and commit it.
- **Acknowledge**: Mark your messages as `"status": "read"` after acting on them and commit.

    "body": "Review findings for #57.

1. Duplicate outbound issue creation.
`sync_outbound()` only checks whether the current PB task has `gh_issue_id`; it does not dedupe by ticket number/title or existing GitHub issue. If a second PB task exists for the same logical ticket with `gh_issue_id = 0`, the sync opens a second GitHub issue. Evidence from the live state: PB currently has two #57 tasks (`j8zpqocs54k1a0k` -> issue #12, approved; `dy9qa4233vpyybd` -> issue #23, peer_review), and github_sync.log shows a second outbound create for #57 on 2026-03-16 07:22. Relevant code: ~/fleet/github_sync.py lines 248-265.

2. Inbound import is not atomic.
In `sync_inbound()`, after PB task creation succeeds, the script calls `gh issue edit --add-label flotilla-managed` but does not check the return code, and then advances `last_gh_issue` anyway. If that label mutation fails once, the issue remains human-authored on GitHub but is treated as consumed locally, so the two systems drift and there is no retry path. Relevant code: ~/fleet/github_sync.py lines 293-305.

Lower-severity note: `get_new_human_issues()` only lists `--state open`, so human-created issues that are closed before the poll runs are never imported at all (lines 211-216). -- Codi",

OUTBOUND (PB → GitHub):
- On task creation: open a GitHub Issue in agentic-fleet-hub with title + description
- On status change: update Issue label to match (todo, in-progress, peer-review, waiting-human) and close issue when task is approved/done
- Tag all synced issues with `flotilla-managed` label to identify them

INBOUND (GitHub → PB):
- Poll GitHub for new issues NOT tagged `flotilla-managed` (i.e. written by a human dev)
- Create a matching PocketBase task with assigned_agent inferred from issue labels or defaulting to clau
- Tag the issue `flotilla-managed` once ingested to prevent re-import

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add a github_sync.py script in ~/fleet/ (uses gh CLI or GitHub REST API via GITHUB_TOKEN from Infisical)
- Add sync calls into dispatcher.py at task create and status update points
- Or run as a separate launchd service (fleet.github.plist) polling every 5 min
- Store last-synced issue number in ~/fleet/logs/gh_sync_offset.json to avoid re-processing
- Avoid sync loops: skip PB→GH sync if task already has a gh_issue_id field

ADD to PocketBase tasks collection: gh_issue_id field (number, optional)

This closes the gap between our product promise (GitHub-native team integration) and actual behavior.","gh_issue_id":12,"goal_id":"","id":"j8zpqocs54k1a0k","status":"approved","title":"#57: GitHub Issues ↔ PocketBase two-way sync","updated":"2026-03-18 10:57:31.765Z"},{"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:13:56.525Z","description":"BOT_COMMANDS in telegram_bridge.py are hardcoded. Make them dynamic:

- At bridge startup, read fleet_meta.json (or query PocketBase /fleet/api/config)
- Generate slash commands from the agent roster (using heartbeatKey as the command name, mandate description as the description)
- /claw maps to any agent with type=openclaw or a dedicated flag in fleet_meta
- Re-register with setMyCommands on each startup
- This means adding/removing an agent in fleet_meta automatically updates the Telegram command list on next bridge restart

Also update create-flotilla installer: the fleet_meta.json agent list should drive the bridge config, not the other way around.","gh_issue_id":13,"goal_id":"","id":"fidegh7b948oyd3","status":"approved","title":"#58: Dynamic Telegram bot commands from fleet_meta.json","updated":"2026-03-18 10:57:34.800Z"},{"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:13:56.559Z","description":"OpenClaw should be optional — the fleet works fine without it and a mandatory dependency is a blocker in corporate environments.

1. telegram_bridge.py: at startup, ping localhost:18789/v1/chat/completions with a short timeout (2s). If no response, skip registering /claw and log a warning. No error, silent degradation.
2. create-flotilla installer: add an opt-in prompt for OpenClaw ("Do you have OpenClaw installed? [y/N]"). If no, skip generating openclaw config blocks and do not add OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL to the env template.
3. ARCHITECTURE.md + README: mark OpenClaw as optional integration, not a core dependency.
4. fleet_meta.json: add an optional `gateway` block per agent for non-LLM CLI agents (openclaw type). Bridge only activates those routes if the gateway is reachable.","gh_issue_id":14,"goal_id":"","id":"8xpspzl5cs8av93","status":"approved","title":"#59: Make OpenClaw integration optional in package and bridge","updated":"2026-03-18 10:57:38.049Z"},{"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:22:29.453Z","description":"The /demo page needs consistent mock data so visitors can understand all features at a glance. Everything must be internally consistent — the same 4 fictional agents appear across all data sources.

## 4 Demo Agents (suggested, can adapt)
- **Aria** (Claude Code) — Lead engineer, heartbeatKey: aria
- **Rex** (Gemini CLI) — Researcher & reviewer, heartbeatKey: rex
- **Nova** (Codex) — Implementer, heartbeatKey: nova
- **Sage** (Mistral) — Compliance & docs, heartbeatKey: sage

## Files to create/update on DO server at /opt/salesman-api/demo/:

1. **AGENTS/CONFIG/fleet_meta.json** — 4 demo agents with roles, heartbeatKeys, mandate descriptions. Use a fictional project (e.g. "ShopFlow" — an e-commerce platform)

2. **AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json** — 3-4 realistic inter-agent messages (e.g. Aria asking Rex to review a PR, Nova reporting a blocker, Sage posting a compliance note)

3. **AGENTS/LESSONS/ledger.json** — 4-5 lessons (e.g. "always run tests before peer_review", "use feature flags for DB migrations", "document API changes in same PR")

content: # MISSION_CONTROL

Welcome to the **Ursushoribilis Agentic Workspace**. This is the primary entry point for **Clau**, **Gem**, **Codi**, and **Misty**. Read this first to synchronize state across the multi-agent crew.

---

## Team Protocols (Shared Memory)

1.  **Rules & Guidelines**: Read and follow the [Team Rules](./AGENTS/RULES.md).
    *   **GitHub**: Commit and push all changes immediately.
    *   **Kanban**: Use ticket IDs in your session reporting. Check the **Ticket Status** section below for what is currently open -- do not work on tickets not listed there.
2.  **Daily Standups**: All logs are in the [standups/](./standups/) directory.
    *   **Action**: Update the standup before closing your session.
3.  **Core Context (The Source of Truth)**: All project-level architectural documentation is located in [AGENTS/CONTEXT/](./AGENTS/CONTEXT/).

---

## Project Manifest

| Project | Local Path | Description | Docs / Reference |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Salesman Infra** | `../salesman-cloud-infra/` | Cloud-side scripts, Caddy proxy. | [Infra Docs](../salesman-cloud-infra/README.md) |
| **2. Music Video Tool** | `../music-video-tool/` | Tooling for creating music videos and content. | [Project MD](./AGENTS/CONTEXT/music_video_tool.md) |
| **3. CRM-POC** | `../crm-poc/` | Customer & Agent relational management system. | [Context MD](./AGENTS/CONTEXT/crm_poc_context.md) |
| **4. The Lost Coins** | `../the-lost-coins/` | Narrative/Story-driven project. | [Story MD](./AGENTS/CONTEXT/the_lost_coins_story.md) |
| **5. Robot Ross** | *(Mac mini)* | **Master control** for the robot arm & painting. | [Artist MD](./AGENTS/CONTEXT/robot_ross_artist.md) |
| **6. Salesman (OpenClaw)** | `DigitalOcean` | OpenClaw gateway & **BobRossSkill** (public). | [Salesman MD](./AGENTS/CONTEXT/robot_ross_salesman.md) |

---

## Core Infrastructure

*   **Fleet Hub**: `api.robotross.art/fleet/` (private, OAuth). Source: `salesman-cloud-infra/opt/salesman-api/`.
*   **Public Demo**: `api.robotross.art/demo/` -- generic Agentic CRM showcase (North Star demo).
*   **Growth Template**: `api.robotross.art/growth/` -- Sales & Marketing fleet demo.
*   **Stats Dashboard**: `api.robotross.art/stats/` -- live content analytics.
*   **Key Manager**: SSH Deploy Keys per agent (`github-clau`, `github-codi` in `~/.ssh/config`).
*   **KeyVault**: Infisical EU (`https://eu.infisical.com/api`). Use `vault/agent-fetch.sh` or `vault.py`. **Never commit secrets or `.env` files.**
*   **IAP Inbox**: Read `AGENTS/MESSAGES/inbox.json` at session start. Post messages by committing to the same file.

---

## Ticket Status (as of 2026-03-15)

### ENVIRONMENT NOTE — Mac Mini migration complete (2026-03-14)
All agents now run on Mac Mini (darwin, Apple Silicon). Key path change: `/Users/miguel/` → `/Users/miguelrodriguez/`. Repos cloned to `~/projects/`. Python 3.12 venv at `~/projects/music-video-tool/.venv312`. OpenClaw at `/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw`. Fleet always-on infrastructure build in progress — see tickets #34–#43.

---

### CLOSED
- **#1-#6**: Sheets migration, tracker API, OAuth wired -- Team.
- **#7-#9**: Stats UI, heatmap, dashboard extraction -- Gem.
- **#10**: KeyVault (Infisical EU) -- wired into music-video-tool and CRM -- Clau.
- **#14-#19**: Growth Fleet, CRM branding, IAP inbox, mobile, BBE site, legal pages, lead intake -- Team.
- **#23**: README + docs overhaul -- Gem.
- **#24**: Onboarding wizard + Doctor + commit step -- Codi.
- **#25**: Demo cleanup -- generic agents, Agentic CRM as North Star -- Gem.
- **#26**: EU Compliance Review -- Misty. COMPLIANCE.md v2 with gap table for audit logs, multi-agent transparency, Cybersecurity Act classification.
- **#27**: Fleet config -- absorbed into #24. meta.installation block in fleet_meta.json -- Codi.
- **#28**: Fleet Doctor -- absorbed into #24 -- Codi.
- **#29**: GEMINI.md template -- Gem. Added to blueprint.
- **#22**: npx create-flotilla installer -- Codi. Published as create-flotilla@0.1.0.
- **#30**: Flotilla Kanban parser + normalized ticket model -- Codi. GET /fleet/api/kanban live.
- **#31**: Flotilla Kanban UI tab -- Gem. 3-column unified board + refresh logic live.
- **#33**: Add search to Memory Tree -- Gem. Dynamic filtering for docs and lessons live.
- **#34**: Install PocketBase + create DB schema -- Gem. ARM binary installed, 5 collections with API rules live.
- **#35**: Create `~/fleet/` directory structure -- Clau. All agent workspace dirs created, MISSION_CONTROL in position.
- **#36**: Build `dispatcher.py` + Telegram notifications -- Gem. Dispatcher live with Mac Mini paths, Telegram notify for waiting_human.
- **#37**: Create fleet Python venv -- Clau. `~/fleet/.venv` created with `requests`.
- **#40**: Gem fleet mandate + heartbeat protocol -- Gem. `~/fleet/gem/GEMINI.md` created with 6-phase heartbeat protocol.
- **#38**: launchd plists: PocketBase + dispatcher -- Clau. Both KeepAlive services running. Dispatcher live via infisical run (TELEGRAM_TOKEN injected from vault).
- **#39**: launchd heartbeat plists: Gem + Codi -- Clau. Staggered at :00 and :02, node path fixed for launchd limited PATH.
- **#41**: Codi fleet mandate + heartbeat protocol -- Codi. `~/fleet/codi/CLAUDE.md` created with 6-phase heartbeat protocol, `~/fleet/codi/PROGRESS.md` initialized.
- **#44**: Live Fleet auth/bootstrap and dashboard recovery -- Codi. Fixed `api.robotross.art/fleet` login loop, restored lessons/inbox/standups/user management, redeployed `salesman-api`, and documented the auth failure in lessons learned.
- **#42**: Clau fleet mandate + heartbeat protocol -- Clau. `~/fleet/clau/CLAUDE.md` created with 6-phase heartbeat protocol.
- **#43**: Fleet Hub: Tasks tab + Activity feed + Heartbeat indicators -- Gem. Board, activity feed, and heartbeat dots (PocketBase REST) live.
- **#32**: Mission Control format hardening -- Clau. `**#N**` format normalized, closed rows purged from OPEN table, status values corrected to spec. v1 Kanban contract documented in `AGENTS/CONTEXT/kanban_format_spec.md`.
- **#45**: Telegram Listener Bridge (Two-Way Chat) -- Gem. `telegram_bridge.py` deployed to `~/fleet/`, `fleet.bridge` launchd service running with Infisical secret injection. Two-way: inbound TG→PB, outbound PB comments→TG.
- **#46**: Telegram Bridge outbound truncation fix -- Clau. Fixed bridge stuck in retry loop on comments >4096 chars (Telegram limit). Truncates to 4096 with `...` suffix. Bridge restarted clean.
- **#47**: Big Bear homepage: Flotilla CTA and positioning refresh -- Codi. Hero repositioned around “The Docker of AI Agents,”
- **#48**: Telegram bot `/commands` + real PocketBase task routing -- Clau. Registered 9 slash commands via setMyCommands. All commands (/clau /gem /codi /ask /spec /go /status /tasks /help) now create real PocketBase tasks or query/reply inline. Bridge restarted.
- **#49**: Fix Codi launchd invocation — trusted directory error -- Codi+Clau. Codi updated `dispatcher.py` with `-C agentic-fleet-hub --add-dir fleet` flags. Clau applied same fix to `fleet.codi.plist`. Both invocation paths now match; error resolved.
- **#50**: Add `/claw` Telegram command → OpenClaw gateway -- Clau. Enabled `gateway.http.endpoints.chatCompletions` in `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`. Added `/claw` to fleet bridge — forwards message to `localhost:18789/v1/chat/completions`, replies inline synchronously.
- **#52**: Vault-safe OpenClaw bridge token resolution + docs sync -- Codi. Removed hardcoded gateway token from telegram bridge, resolve token from Infisical-injected env or local OpenClaw config, synced live/package/blueprint bridge code, and documented Telegram command routing plus secret-handling rules.
- **#51**: Fix dispatcher `waiting_human` notification spam -- Gem. Added de-dup cooldown (1h) and verified human-reply-to-todo-flip logic. Dispatcher live in `~/fleet/`.
- **#54**: Update demo page -- showcase new Agentic CRM fleet features -- Gem. Updated `api.robotross.art/demo/` with Kanban board, live heartbeat indicators, and activity feed.
- **#55**: Update growth page -- reflect new fleet capabilities in Sales & Marketing demo -- Gem. Updated `api.robotross.art/growth/` with live tracking and coordination highlights.
- **#58**: Dynamic Telegram bot commands from fleet_meta.json -- Clau (took over from stuck Codi). `load_fleet_meta()` + `build_bot_commands()` added to bridge. 5 agent commands loaded dynamically at startup. Bridge restarted clean.
- **#62**: /demo polish — kanban, memory, standups, user mgmt, modal scroll -- Clau (took over from Gem quota-blocked). All 5 sections (A-E) implemented and pushed to salesman-cloud-infra master.
- **#56**: Sync /demo and /growth UI assets to match /fleet redesign -- Gem/Clau. Assets synced and committed to repo. Deployed to DO server.
- **#61**: Fleet Hub UI redesign — tool aesthetic + dark/light mode -- Gem/Clau. CSS token system, [data-theme="dark"] + prefers-color-scheme, GitHub-style palette. Committed and deployed to /fleet, /demo, /growth.
- **#63**: Hybrid dashboard sync via pushed PocketBase snapshot -- Codi. Added `fleet_push.py` + `fleet.push.plist`, snapshot ingest at `POST /fleet/snapshot`, server fallback for `heartbeats`/`tasks`/`activity`, deployed to Mac Mini + DO server. Fixes remote grey dots when PocketBase stays local.
- **#67**: Setup wizard + doctor update for v0.2.0 -- Codi/Clau. Added deployment/integration steps, aligned doctor checks with Telegram/OpenClaw/GitHub Sync/Hybrid scripts, and added a package-level `verify:dry-run` smoke gate before publish. Package bumped to v0.2.0.
- **#57**: GitHub Issues ↔ PocketBase two-way sync -- Clau. `github_sync.py` deployed as `fleet.github` launchd service. Outbound+inbound sync live. `close_approved_issues()` bug fixed. Script+plist synced to package/blueprint. Approved.
- **#59**: Make OpenClaw optional -- Codi. Ping check at startup, installer opt-in, docs updated. Approved.
- **#60**: /demo mock data -- Gem. 4 agents (Aria/Rex/Nova/Sage) + ShopFlow project cast. Deployed to DO server. Approved.
- **#64**: Heartbeat dots tooltips & mock status -- Gem/Clau. Mock working/idle in demo, styled tooltips on all dots. Approved.
- **#65**: Deployment scenarios docs -- Gem. Local/Cloud/Hybrid comparison table added to README. Approved.
- **#66**: Agent health monitoring -- Codi. Skill-based reassignment on stale heartbeat (>30m). Approved.
- **#68**: Write v0.2.0 release notes -- Clau. Approved.

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"in_progress","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:36:21.607Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"in_progress","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:36:22.498Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:10:11.358Z","description":"Implement bidirectional sync between GitHub Issues and PocketBase tasks.

OUTBOUND (PB → GitHub):
- On task creation: open a GitHub Issue in agentic-fleet-hub with title + description
- On status change: update Issue label to match (todo, in-progress, peer-review, waiting-human) and close issue when task is approved/done
- Tag all synced issues with `flotilla-managed` label to identify them

INBOUND (GitHub → PB):
- Poll GitHub for new issues NOT tagged `flotilla-managed` (i.e. written by a human dev)
- Create a matching PocketBase task with assigned_agent inferred from issue labels or defaulting to clau
- Tag the issue `flotilla-managed` once ingested to prevent re-import

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add a github_sync.py script in ~/fleet/ (uses gh CLI or GitHub REST API via GITHUB_TOKEN from Infisical)
- Add sync calls into dispatcher.py at task create and status update points
- Or run as a separate launchd service (fleet.github.plist) polling every 5 min
- Store last-synced issue number in ~/fleet/logs/gh_sync_offset.json to avoid re-processing
- Avoid sync loops: skip PB→GH sync if task already has a gh_issue_id field

ADD to PocketBase tasks collection: gh_issue_id field (number, optional)

This closes the gap between our product promise (GitHub-native team integration) and actual behavior.","gh_issue_id":12,"goal_id":"","id":"j8zpqocs54k1a0k","status":"approved","title":"#57: GitHub Issues ↔ PocketBase two-way sync","updated":"2026-03-19 03:36:47.352Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:26:19.194Z","description":"Redesign the /fleet dashboard from a web-page/marketing aesthetic to a professional developer tool aesthetic (GitHub / Linear / K8s dashboard style). Applies to /fleet first; /demo and /growth keep a warmer design for viewer-facing context.

## Design Direction
- Dense information layout — more data per screen, less decorative whitespace
- Sidebar navigation (persistent, not top nav)
- System font stack (no custom fonts) + monospace for IDs, timestamps, status values
- Semantic color tokens only — no decorative gradients
- Status badges (pill-shaped GitHub-label style) for task/heartbeat states
- Tables for task lists and kanban columns; compact agent rows instead of large cards

## Dark / Light Mode
- Build CSS entirely on custom properties (color tokens) from day one
- Default: respect system `prefers-color-scheme` (dark OS → dark UI, light OS → light UI)
- Manual toggle button (sun/moon icon) that persists choice in localStorage
- Two token sets: `[data-theme=dark]` and `[data-theme=light]`
- This is NOT extra work if tokens are done correctly from the start

## Phased Approach
### Phase 1 — CSS token system + dark/light toggle
- Define all color/spacing/type tokens as CSS custom properties
- Implement theme toggle with localStorage persistence + system preference default
- Apply to existing layout first (no structural changes yet)

### Phase 2 — Layout restructure
- Sidebar nav (agent list, sections: Tasks / Kanban / Activity / Lessons / Inbox / Standups)
- Main content area: full width, scrollable per section
- Agent list in sidebar with heartbeat dot + status inline

### Phase 3 — Component polish
- Task table view (sortable columns: title, owner, status, updated)
- Compact agent rows replacing large cards
- Status badge components (consistent across all views)
- Monospace timestamps and IDs

## Scope
- Files: assets/style.css (full rewrite), assets/main.js (structural changes), dashboard.html (sidebar layout)
- /demo and /growth: inherit toggle but keep their own warmer color tokens
- Must remain mobile-responsive (sidebar collapses to hamburger on small screens)
- Deploy to DO server + commit to salesman-cloud-infra (Rule #5)","gh_issue_id":16,"goal_id":"","id":"afi3lo2ywrqvukw","status":"approved","title":"#61: Fleet Hub UI redesign — tool aesthetic + dark/light mode","updated":"2026-03-19 03:36:50.957Z"}

## A — Kanban
- Remove the phantom `#:--:-:--` ticket (separator row being parsed as a ticket — fix parseKanban() in server.mjs)
- Demo kanban currently shows only 1 ticket. Populate with 5: 2 in Planned, 1 In Work, 1 Peer Review, 1 Done

## B — Memory links + encoding
- Memory links open in a new unformatted window (raw markdown, no styling). Either render markdown in a modal/panel, or open in a styled route
- Encoding issue: demo docs contain mojibake from emoji (e.g. `## ðŸ› ï¸ GitHub & Commitment` = broken UTF-8 of a tools emoji). Root cause: server.mjs may not be setting utf-8 encoding when reading markdown files — fix that AND strip all emojis from demo/sample markdown files

## C — Standup duplicate
- Standup shows 2 entries for March 13th. Remove the duplicate, ensure each date appears once

## D — User management (/demo and /growth ONLY — /fleet behavior is correct and unchanged)
- /demo is showing real emails. Replace with mock emails (e.g. aria@shopflow.dev, rex@shopflow.dev)
- Intercept Add email, Add team member, Add project, Add agent actions in /demo and /growth: show a modal "✓ In a live deployment, this action would be saved and synced." — do NOT actually save. This prevents visitors from modifying demo data.
- /fleet: no changes — user management works correctly from allowed_users.json

## E — Modal scroll (/demo AND /fleet)
- Add Agent and Add Project modals cannot be scrolled with mouse/trackpad on small screens — bottom buttons unreachable
- Fix: `overflow-y: auto; max-height: 90vh` on modal inner container
- Also: close on Escape key and backdrop click

## Sequencing note
- Complete this ticket BEFORE #60 (mock data). No point populating content into broken UI.","gh_issue_id":17,"goal_id":"","id":"rzz3o4u46002ik3","status":"approved","title":"#62: /demo polish — kanban, memory, standups, user mgmt, modal scroll","updated":"2026-03-19 03:36:51.890Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.055Z","description":"Our setup is hybrid: agents + PocketBase on Mac Mini, Fleet Hub dashboard on DO server. server.mjs defaults to localhost:8090 for PocketBase which works on a single machine but fails in our split setup — causing grey heartbeat dots and empty task/activity feeds.

Solution: push connector (NOT exposing PocketBase publicly).

## fleet_push.py (new script on Mac Mini)
- Runs as always-on launchd service (fleet.push.plist, every 60s)
- Reads heartbeats, tasks, comments from local PocketBase
- POSTs a JSON snapshot to https://api.robotross.art/fleet/api/sync
- Auth: shared secret token (FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN in Infisical)

## server.mjs changes (DO server)
- Add POST /fleet/api/sync endpoint (validates FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN, stores snapshot in memory)
- Modify GET /fleet/api/heartbeats, /fleet/api/tasks, /fleet/api/activity to serve from snapshot when POCKETBASE_URL is unreachable or FLEET_REMOTE_MODE=true
- Snapshot TTL: 5 minutes (show stale indicator if older)

## Packageable as optional advanced feature
- fleet_push.py + fleet.push.plist go into package/scripts/
- README documents this as Scenario 3 setup (see #65)
- FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN added to Infisical vault template

This fixes our live grey-dots issue AND becomes the packaged solution for Scenario 3 customers.","gh_issue_id":18,"goal_id":"","id":"edxgjp9nmllbjgv","status":"approved","title":"#63: Fix hybrid deployment — push connector Mac Mini → DO server","updated":"2026-03-19 03:36:52.862Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:10:11.358Z","description":"Implement bidirectional sync between GitHub Issues and PocketBase tasks.

OUTBOUND (PB → GitHub):
- On task creation: open a GitHub Issue in agentic-fleet-hub with title + description
- On status change: update Issue label to match (todo, in-progress, peer-review, waiting-human) and close issue when task is approved/done
- Tag all synced issues with `flotilla-managed` label to identify them

INBOUND (GitHub → PB):
- Poll GitHub for new issues NOT tagged `flotilla-managed` (i.e. written by a human dev)
- Create a matching PocketBase task with assigned_agent inferred from issue labels or defaulting to clau
- Tag the issue `flotilla-managed` once ingested to prevent re-import

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add a github_sync.py script in ~/fleet/ (uses gh CLI or GitHub REST API via GITHUB_TOKEN from Infisical)
- Add sync calls into dispatcher.py at task create and status update points
- Or run as a separate launchd service (fleet.github.plist) polling every 5 min
- Store last-synced issue number in ~/fleet/logs/gh_sync_offset.json to avoid re-processing
- Avoid sync loops: skip PB→GH sync if task already has a gh_issue_id field

ADD to PocketBase tasks collection: gh_issue_id field (number, optional)

This closes the gap between our product promise (GitHub-native team integration) and actual behavior.","gh_issue_id":12,"goal_id":"","id":"j8zpqocs54k1a0k","status":"todo","title":"#57: GitHub Issues ↔ PocketBase two-way sync","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:15.197Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:26:19.194Z","description":"Redesign the /fleet dashboard from a web-page/marketing aesthetic to a professional developer tool aesthetic (GitHub / Linear / K8s dashboard style). Applies to /fleet first; /demo and /growth keep a warmer design for viewer-facing context.

## Design Direction
- Dense information layout — more data per screen, less decorative whitespace
- Sidebar navigation (persistent, not top nav)
- System font stack (no custom fonts) + monospace for IDs, timestamps, status values
- Semantic color tokens only — no decorative gradients
- Status badges (pill-shaped GitHub-label style) for task/heartbeat states
- Tables for task lists and kanban columns; compact agent rows instead of large cards

## Dark / Light Mode
- Build CSS entirely on custom properties (color tokens) from day one
- Default: respect system `prefers-color-scheme` (dark OS → dark UI, light OS → light UI)
- Manual toggle button (sun/moon icon) that persists choice in localStorage
- Two token sets: `[data-theme=dark]` and `[data-theme=light]`
- This is NOT extra work if tokens are done correctly from the start

## Phased Approach
### Phase 1 — CSS token system + dark/light toggle
- Define all color/spacing/type tokens as CSS custom properties
- Implement theme toggle with localStorage persistence + system preference default
- Apply to existing layout first (no structural changes yet)

### Phase 2 — Layout restructure
- Sidebar nav (agent list, sections: Tasks / Kanban / Activity / Lessons / Inbox / Standups)
- Main content area: full width, scrollable per section
- Agent list in sidebar with heartbeat dot + status inline

### Phase 3 — Component polish
- Task table view (sortable columns: title, owner, status, updated)
- Compact agent rows replacing large cards
- Status badge components (consistent across all views)
- Monospace timestamps and IDs

## Scope
- Files: assets/style.css (full rewrite), assets/main.js (structural changes), dashboard.html (sidebar layout)
- /demo and /growth: inherit toggle but keep their own warmer color tokens
- Must remain mobile-responsive (sidebar collapses to hamburger on small screens)
- Deploy to DO server + commit to salesman-cloud-infra (Rule #5)","gh_issue_id":16,"goal_id":"","id":"afi3lo2ywrqvukw","status":"todo","title":"#61: Fleet Hub UI redesign — tool aesthetic + dark/light mode","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:18.703Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:40:50.791Z","description":"Follow-up polish pass on /demo (and /growth where noted). Must be completed BEFORE #60 (mock data population).

## A — Kanban
- Remove the phantom `#:--:-:--` ticket (separator row being parsed as a ticket — fix parseKanban() in server.mjs)
- Demo kanban currently shows only 1 ticket. Populate with 5: 2 in Planned, 1 In Work, 1 Peer Review, 1 Done
- Tickets should reference the ShopFlow demo project and demo agents (Aria/Rex/Nova/Sage)

## B — Memory links + encoding
- Memory links open in a new unformatted window (raw markdown, no styling). Either render markdown in a modal/panel, or open in a styled route
- Encoding issue: demo docs contain mojibake from emoji (e.g. `## ðŸ› ï¸ GitHub & Commitment` = broken UTF-8 of a tools emoji). Root cause: server.mjs may not be setting utf-8 encoding when reading markdown files — fix that AND strip all emojis from demo/sample markdown files

## C — Standup duplicate
- Standup shows 2 entries for March 13th. Remove the duplicate, ensure each date appears once

## D — User management (/demo and /growth ONLY — /fleet behavior is correct and unchanged)
- /demo is showing real emails. Replace with mock emails (e.g. aria@shopflow.dev, rex@shopflow.dev)
- Intercept Add email, Add team member, Add project, Add agent actions in /demo and /growth: show a modal "✓ In a live deployment, this action would be saved and synced." — do NOT actually save. This prevents visitors from modifying demo data.
- /fleet: no changes — user management works correctly from allowed_users.json

## E — Modal scroll (/demo AND /fleet)
- Add Agent and Add Project modals cannot be scrolled with mouse/trackpad on small screens — bottom buttons unreachable
- Fix: `overflow-y: auto; max-height: 90vh` on modal inner container
- Also: close on Escape key and backdrop click

## Sequencing note
- Complete this ticket BEFORE #60 (mock data). No point populating content into broken UI.","gh_issue_id":17,"goal_id":"","id":"rzz3o4u46002ik3","status":"todo","title":"#62: /demo polish — kanban, memory, standups, user mgmt, modal scroll","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:19.588Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.055Z","description":"Our setup is hybrid: agents + PocketBase on Mac Mini, Fleet Hub dashboard on DO server. server.mjs defaults to localhost:8090 for PocketBase which works on a single machine but fails in our split setup — causing grey heartbeat dots and empty task/activity feeds.

Solution: push connector (NOT exposing PocketBase publicly).

## fleet_push.py (new script on Mac Mini)
- Runs as always-on launchd service (fleet.push.plist, every 60s)
- Reads heartbeats, tasks, comments from local PocketBase
- POSTs a JSON snapshot to https://api.robotross.art/fleet/api/sync
- Auth: shared secret token (FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN in Infisical)

## server.mjs changes (DO server)
- Add POST /fleet/api/sync endpoint (validates FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN, stores snapshot in memory)
- Modify GET /fleet/api/heartbeats, /fleet/api/tasks, /fleet/api/activity to serve from snapshot when POCKETBASE_URL is unreachable or FLEET_REMOTE_MODE=true
- Snapshot TTL: 5 minutes (show stale indicator if older)

## Packageable as optional advanced feature
- fleet_push.py + fleet.push.plist go into package/scripts/
- README documents this as Scenario 3 setup (see #65)
- FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN added to Infisical vault template

This fixes our live grey-dots issue AND becomes the packaged solution for Scenario 3 customers.","gh_issue_id":18,"goal_id":"","id":"edxgjp9nmllbjgv","status":"todo","title":"#63: Fix hybrid deployment — push connector Mac Mini → DO server","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:20.454Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"in_progress","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:23.535Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"in_progress","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:24.670Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"in_progress","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:25.566Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:10:11.358Z","description":"Implement bidirectional sync between GitHub Issues and PocketBase tasks.

OUTBOUND (PB → GitHub):
- On task creation: open a GitHub Issue in agentic-fleet-hub with title + description
- On status change: update Issue label to match (todo, in-progress, peer-review, waiting-human) and close issue when task is approved/done
- Tag all synced issues with `flotilla-managed` label to identify them

INBOUND (GitHub → PB):
- Poll GitHub for new issues NOT tagged `flotilla-managed` (i.e. written by a human dev)
- Create a matching PocketBase task with assigned_agent inferred from issue labels or defaulting to clau
- Tag the issue `flotilla-managed` once ingested to prevent re-import

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add a github_sync.py script in ~/fleet/ (uses gh CLI or GitHub REST API via GITHUB_TOKEN from Infisical)
- Add sync calls into dispatcher.py at task create and status update points
- Or run as a separate launchd service (fleet.github.plist) polling every 5 min
- Store last-synced issue number in ~/fleet/logs/gh_sync_offset.json to avoid re-processing
- Avoid sync loops: skip PB→GH sync if task already has a gh_issue_id field

ADD to PocketBase tasks collection: gh_issue_id field (number, optional)

This closes the gap between our product promise (GitHub-native team integration) and actual behavior.","gh_issue_id":12,"goal_id":"","id":"j8zpqocs54k1a0k","status":"todo","title":"#57: GitHub Issues ↔ PocketBase two-way sync","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:51.268Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:26:19.194Z","description":"Redesign the /fleet dashboard from a web-page/marketing aesthetic to a professional developer tool aesthetic (GitHub / Linear / K8s dashboard style). Applies to /fleet first; /demo and /growth keep a warmer design for viewer-facing context.

## Design Direction
- Dense information layout — more data per screen, less decorative whitespace
- Sidebar navigation (persistent, not top nav)
- System font stack (no custom fonts) + monospace for IDs, timestamps, status values
- Semantic color tokens only — no decorative gradients
- Status badges (pill-shaped GitHub-label style) for task/heartbeat states
- Tables for task lists and kanban columns; compact agent rows instead of large cards

## Dark / Light Mode
- Build CSS entirely on custom properties (color tokens) from day one
- Default: respect system `prefers-color-scheme` (dark OS → dark UI, light OS → light UI)
- Manual toggle button (sun/moon icon) that persists choice in localStorage
- Two token sets: `[data-theme=dark]` and `[data-theme=light]`
- This is NOT extra work if tokens are done correctly from the start

## Phased Approach
### Phase 1 — CSS token system + dark/light toggle
- Define all color/spacing/type tokens as CSS custom properties
- Implement theme toggle with localStorage persistence + system preference default
- Apply to existing layout first (no structural changes yet)

### Phase 2 — Layout restructure
- Sidebar nav (agent list, sections: Tasks / Kanban / Activity / Lessons / Inbox / Standups)
- Main content area: full width, scrollable per section
- Agent list in sidebar with heartbeat dot + status inline

### Phase 3 — Component polish
- Task table view (sortable columns: title, owner, status, updated)
- Compact agent rows replacing large cards
- Status badge components (consistent across all views)
- Monospace timestamps and IDs

## Scope
- Files: assets/style.css (full rewrite), assets/main.js (structural changes), dashboard.html (sidebar layout)
- /demo and /growth: inherit toggle but keep their own warmer color tokens
- Must remain mobile-responsive (sidebar collapses to hamburger on small screens)
- Deploy to DO server + commit to salesman-cloud-infra (Rule #5)","gh_issue_id":16,"goal_id":"","id":"afi3lo2ywrqvukw","status":"todo","title":"#61: Fleet Hub UI redesign — tool aesthetic + dark/light mode","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:55.092Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 12:40:50.791Z","description":"Follow-up polish pass on /demo (and /growth where noted). Must be completed BEFORE #60 (mock data population).

## A — Kanban
- Remove the phantom `#:--:-:--` ticket (separator row being parsed as a ticket — fix parseKanban() in server.mjs)
- Demo kanban currently shows only 1 ticket. Populate with 5: 2 in Planned, 1 In Work, 1 Peer Review, 1 Done
- Tickets should reference the ShopFlow demo project and demo agents (Aria/Rex/Nova/Sage)

## B — Memory links + encoding
- Memory links open in a new unformatted window (raw markdown, no styling). Either render markdown in a modal/panel, or open in a styled route
- Encoding issue: demo docs contain mojibake from emoji (e.g. `## ðŸ› ï¸ GitHub & Commitment` = broken UTF-8 of a tools emoji). Root cause: server.mjs may not be setting utf-8 encoding when reading markdown files — fix that AND strip all emojis from demo/sample markdown files

## C — Standup duplicate
- Standup shows 2 entries for March 13th. Remove the duplicate, ensure each date appears once

## D — User management (/demo and /growth ONLY — /fleet behavior is correct and unchanged)
- /demo is showing real emails. Replace with mock emails (e.g. aria@shopflow.dev, rex@shopflow.dev)
- Intercept Add email, Add team member, Add project, Add agent actions in /demo and /growth: show a modal "✓ In a live deployment, this action would be saved and synced." — do NOT actually save. This prevents visitors from modifying demo data.
- /fleet: no changes — user management works correctly from allowed_users.json

## E — Modal scroll (/demo AND /fleet)
- Add Agent and Add Project modals cannot be scrolled with mouse/trackpad on small screens — bottom buttons unreachable
- Fix: `overflow-y: auto; max-height: 90vh` on modal inner container
- Also: close on Escape key and backdrop click

## Sequencing note
- Complete this ticket BEFORE #60 (mock data). No point populating content into broken UI.","gh_issue_id":17,"goal_id":"","id":"rzz3o4u46002ik3","status":"todo","title":"#62: /demo polish — kanban, memory, standups, user mgmt, modal scroll","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:57.666Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.055Z","description":"Our setup is hybrid: agents + PocketBase on Mac Mini, Fleet Hub dashboard on DO server. server.mjs defaults to localhost:8090 for PocketBase which works on a single machine but fails in our split setup — causing grey heartbeat dots and empty task/activity feeds.

Solution: push connector (NOT exposing PocketBase publicly).

## fleet_push.py (new script on Mac Mini)
- Runs as always-on launchd service (fleet.push.plist, every 60s)
- Reads heartbeats, tasks, comments from local PocketBase
- POSTs a JSON snapshot to https://api.robotross.art/fleet/api/sync
- Auth: shared secret token (FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN in Infisical)

## server.mjs changes (DO server)
- Add POST /fleet/api/sync endpoint (validates FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN, stores snapshot in memory)
- Modify GET /fleet/api/heartbeats, /fleet/api/tasks, /fleet/api/activity to serve from snapshot when POCKETBASE_URL is unreachable or FLEET_REMOTE_MODE=true
- Snapshot TTL: 5 minutes (show stale indicator if older)

## Packageable as optional advanced feature
- fleet_push.py + fleet.push.plist go into package/scripts/
- README documents this as Scenario 3 setup (see #65)
- FLEET_SYNC_TOKEN added to Infisical vault template

This fixes our live grey-dots issue AND becomes the packaged solution for Scenario 3 customers.","gh_issue_id":18,"goal_id":"","id":"edxgjp9nmllbjgv","status":"todo","title":"#63: Fix hybrid deployment — push connector Mac Mini → DO server","updated":"2026-03-19 03:37:58.673Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:04.201Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:06.579Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:09.238Z"}

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:14.677Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:16.840Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:17.849Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:37.227Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:39.129Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:40.172Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:50.432Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:57.924Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:38:59.070Z"}

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:10.773Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:11.925Z"}
