stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:14.836Z"}

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:36.680Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:37.827Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:40.234Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:39:54.307Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:40:13.436Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:40:16.547Z"}

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:40:38.328Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:40:41.577Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:40:44.477Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:41:12.647Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:41:14.131Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:41:15.429Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:41:25.078Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:41:42.746Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:41:45.537Z"}

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:42:09.674Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:42:12.359Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:42:14.641Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:42:47.669Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:42:50.108Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:42:53.093Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:43:14.080Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"clau","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.084Z","description":"Two small improvements to heartbeat dots across all fleet pages.

## A — Mock status in /demo and /growth
- /demo and /growth have no live PocketBase connection, so all dots show grey
- In demo/growth mode, generate mock heartbeat status from fleet_meta agent list: alternate working/idle to look realistic (e.g. 2 working, 2 idle)
- Detection: if /fleet/api/heartbeats returns empty or errors, fall back to mock status
- Do NOT do this for /fleet — grey there is accurate and meaningful

## B — Tooltip on heartbeat dots (all pages including /fleet)
- Each dot already has a title= attribute — make it visible as a proper styled tooltip on hover
- Tooltip content: agent name + status + last seen (e.g. "Gem — working · 2m ago")
- For /demo: "Aria — working (demo)" to be transparent it is mock data
- Tooltip should work on mobile too (tap to show)","gh_issue_id":19,"goal_id":"","id":"qzoe7wv9xb7ro1u","status":"todo","title":"#64: Heartbeat dots — mock status in /demo+/growth, tooltip on all","updated":"2026-03-19 03:43:29.787Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:43:32.183Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"gem","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:02:31.111Z","description":"Add a deployment scenarios section to the README or a dedicated DEPLOYMENT.md doc. Three scenarios must be clearly explained so customers self-select the right setup.

## Scenario 1 — Local (default)
- Everything on one machine: agents + PocketBase + Fleet Hub
- Access dashboard at localhost:8787/fleet/
- Zero extra config — works out of the box with create-flotilla
- Best for: personal use, local teams, privacy-first corporate installs

## Scenario 2 — Cloud VPS (single server)
- Everything on one cloud VPS (DigitalOcean, AWS EC2, etc.)
- Dashboard publicly accessible at https://your-domain.com/fleet/
- PocketBase and Fleet Hub are co-located, no connectivity gap
- Best for: remote teams, always-on fleets, public dashboards
- Note: Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Codex must support headless operation on Linux

## Scenario 3 — Hybrid (agents local, dashboard remote)
- Agents + PocketBase on a local machine (Mac Mini, on-prem server)
- Fleet Hub hosted publicly on a separate cloud server
- Requires the push connector (fleet_push.py — see #63)
- Best for: hardware-connected agents (robot arms, local files), enterprise privacy + public visibility
- CTA: "Scenario 3 requires additional configuration. Contact us at miguel@bigbearengineering.com for setup assistance."

## Placement
- Add as a section in the main README under Getting Started
- Or as a standalone docs/DEPLOYMENT.md linked from README
- Keep it scannable: use a comparison table + short paragraph per scenario","gh_issue_id":20,"goal_id":"","id":"v36k29yjdsvulou","status":"todo","title":"#65: Document 3 deployment scenarios in getting-started guide","updated":"2026-03-19 03:43:54.204Z"}

stdout: {"assigned_agent":"codi","collectionId":"tw44vd067pqost4","collectionName":"tasks","created":"2026-03-15 13:08:17.789Z","description":"When an agent goes offline (token exhaustion, crash, etc.) their todo tasks should be automatically reassigned to the best available substitute based on skill overlap.

## Detection (dispatcher.py)
- Every dispatch cycle, check heartbeat age for each agent that has pending todo tasks
- If last heartbeat > 30 min (3 missed cycles): mark as suspected_offline
- Log + send Telegram: "⚠️ Gem appears offline (last seen 35m ago). Reassigning 2 tasks."

## Reassignment logic
- Get all todo tasks assigned to offline agent
- For each task, score all active agents by skill overlap:
  score = len(intersection(agent.skills, task.required_skills or offline_agent.skills))
- Assign to highest-scoring available agent
- Post a comment on the task: "Reassigned from gem to clau (gem offline, skill match: Infrastructure-as-Code)"
- Notify Telegram with full reassignment summary

## fleet_meta.json changes
- skills already exists on all agents — no schema change needed
- Add optional fallbackChain per agent (ordered list of preferred substitutes) for cases where skill matching is ambiguous
  e.g. "fallbackChain": ["clau", "codi"]

## PocketBase tasks collection
- Add optional required_skills field (array of strings)
- Dispatcher and Fleet Hub task creation form both support it
- If empty, use offline agent skills as proxy for matching

## Fleet Hub UI (assets/main.js + dashboard.html)
- Agent cards: show skills as editable chips (click to remove, + to add)
- Task creation form: optional required_skills tag input
- Agent card: show suspected_offline state visually (amber dot + tooltip "Last seen Xm ago — may be out of tokens")

## Recovery
- When a suspected_offline agent posts a heartbeat again, log recovery
- Do NOT auto-reassign back — human or agent can manually reassign if desired
- Telegram: "✅ Gem is back online (was offline 45m)."","gh_issue_id":21,"goal_id":"","id":"kyac82r341kj122","status":"todo","title":"#66: Agent health monitoring + skill-based task reassignment","updated":"2026-03-19 03:43:57.145Z"}
